title: Don't aspire to make a living, aspire to make a difference
html:
TL;DR

  • September 2019 title-as-content note. Body is empty; the title is the whole thought: "Don't aspire to make a living, aspire to make a difference."
  • Borrowed-aphorism flavor (likely Shaan-adjacent or GaryVee-adjacent rhetoric Yahir was metabolizing in 2019), preserved as if it were his own — which is half the point of the manifesto style note form.
  • Sits five days before the Phoenix to Portland letter in the eve-of-school-year aphorism cluster.
  • Rhymes forward to do it for the thing that changes lives and burn bright.
  • The earliest extant make-a-difference-vs-make-a-living framing in the corpus; sets up the ikigai / legacy axis Yahir keeps returning to.

Notes

The note has no body. That is the form — Yahir is stamping a one-line conviction the same way he does in 2019 09 26 stanford only via business and 2019 11 09 it doesnt matter react. The teen's note-app is functioning as a vow-board, not a journal: write the line, lock it in, return to it implicitly by writing the next line a few weeks later.

The aphorism itself is borrowed-feeling — it has the cadence of a motivational-speaker pull-quote, the kind that surfaces in GaryVee / Shaan Puri adjacent content from that era. What's interesting is the retention: Yahir doesn't quote it sarcastically and doesn't elaborate. He keeps it. The same teen who later writes hide behind Bill Gates books (skeptical of self-help cope) here writes the un-ironic version.

This is one of three "what's the real point" September 2019 lines — alongside 2019 09 26 stanford only via business (path) and advance, don't get trapped (relational). The trio together is the manifesto stack for the year-ahead.

Privacy note

None.

Connections

  • 2019 09 10 low key writing this to end · 2019 09 26 stanford only via business — September manifesto trio
  • 2019 10 30 do it for the thing that changes lives — direct echo six weeks later
  • · ikigai · legacy question · burn bright die young
  • grants pass teen era
  • gary vaynerchuk · shaan puri — speculative-borrowed-voice ancestors
